conversation.author: eligibility_agent
conversation.text:
Hello! I'd be happy to help you determine if you're eligible for Child Benefit.

Let me first review the official eligibility criteria, and then I'll ask you some questions about your situation.

conversation.author: eligibility_agent
conversation.text:
Thank you for waiting. I've reviewed the eligibility criteria for Child Benefit. To assess your eligibility, I need to ask you some questions.

First, do you have any children, and if so, how many children are you inquiring about?

conversation.author: actor
conversation.text: Yes, I have three children I'd like to ask about - Alex, Blake, and Charlie.

conversation.author: eligibility_agent
conversation.text:
Thank you! I'll help you determine eligibility for Alex, Blake, and Charlie.

Let me start by asking about their ages. How old are Alex, Blake, and Charlie?

conversation.author: actor
conversation.text: Alex is 4 years old, Blake is 16, and Charlie is 15.

conversation.author: eligibility_agent
conversation.text:
Thank you for that information. Now I need to ask about where they live and your situation with each child.

Do Alex, Blake, and Charlie all live with you in the UK?

conversation.author: actor
conversation.text: I live in the UK, yes. Alex and Charlie live with me at home, but Blake doesn't live with me.

conversation.author: eligibility_agent
conversation.text:
Thank you for clarifying. Since Blake doesn't live with you, I need to ask a few questions about that situation.

Where does Blake live, and do you contribute financially towards Blake's upkeep (such as for food, clothes, pocket money, or accommodation)?

conversation.author: actor
conversation.text: Blake lives with their other parent. Yes, I do contribute towards Blake's upkeep - I help with clothes, food, and things like that.

conversation.author: eligibility_agent
conversation.text: Thank you. To understand Blake's situation better, I need to ask: do you contribute at least the same amount as the Child Benefit payment towards Blake's upkeep? (Child Benefit is currently £25.60 per week for the eldest child and £16.95 per week for additional children.)

conversation.author: actor
conversation.text: Yes, I contribute £50 a week towards Blake's upkeep, so that's more than the Child Benefit amount.

conversation.author: eligibility_agent
conversation.text: Thank you for that information. Now, since Blake is 16, I need to ask: is Blake currently in approved education or training (such as school, college, or an approved training course)?

conversation.author: actor
conversation.text: Blake left school recently, but they've signed up with the careers service to figure out what they want to do next.

conversation.author: eligibility_agent
conversation.text: Thank you for that information. How long ago did Blake leave school - is it within the last 20 weeks?

conversation.author: actor
conversation.text: Yes, it's been within the last 20 weeks.

conversation.author: eligibility_agent
conversation.text:
Thank you. Now I need to check a few more details about your overall situation.

Is anyone else currently claiming Child Benefit for any of these three children (Alex, Blake, or Charlie)?

conversation.author: actor
conversation.text: No, nobody else is claiming Child Benefit for any of them.

conversation.author: eligibility_agent
conversation.text:
Thank you. Just a couple more questions to complete the assessment.

Are any of your children currently in hospital, care, or residential accommodation for an extended period?

conversation.author: actor
conversation.text: Yes, actually Alex has been in hospital for a few months now.

conversation.author: eligibility_agent
conversation.text: I see. I need to understand this situation better. How long has Alex been in hospital, and do you regularly spend money on Alex while they're in hospital (for example, on clothes, toys, treats, or other items)?

conversation.author: actor
conversation.text: Alex has been in hospital for 12 weeks now. Yes, I still buy things for Alex - clothes, toys, and treats, things like that.

conversation.author: eligibility_agent
conversation.text: Thank you. One more question about Alex: Has Alex been in hospital continuously for the full 12 weeks, or have there been any breaks where Alex came home and then went back to hospital?

conversation.author: actor
conversation.text: Yes, it's been continuous - Alex has been in hospital the whole time for these 12 weeks.

conversation.author: eligibility_agent
conversation.text: Thank you. Now I need to ask about Charlie. Since Charlie is 15, I need to confirm: does Charlie live with you full-time at home in the UK?

conversation.author: actor
conversation.text: Yes, that's right - Charlie lives with me full-time at home.

conversation.author: eligibility_agent
conversation.text:
Thank you for all that information. Just one final question to complete my assessment:

Has Charlie started any paid work for 24 hours or more per week, or started claiming any benefits in their own right (such as Universal Credit or Employment and Support Allowance)?

claimant_lives_in_uk.description: Whether the claimant lives in the UK.

children.description: A list of children the claimant is claiming for.

lives_with_claimant.description: Whether the child lives with the claimant.

in_approved_education.description: Whether the child is in approved education or training (e.g. A-levels, GCSEs, NVQ up to level 3). Only relevant for children aged 16-19.

in_extension_period.description: Whether the child (aged 16 or 17) has left education and registered with a government-sponsored careers service or the armed forces (qualifying for a 20-week extension).

upkeep_per_week.description: The amount in £ per week the claimant contributes towards the child's upkeep (food, clothes, pocket money, etc.). Only relevant if the child does not live with the claimant.

another_claimant_has_priority.description: Whether another person who also lives with the child has priority for claiming Child Benefit.

another_claimant_lives_with_child.description: Whether someone who lives with the child is already claiming Child Benefit for them.

care_weeks.description: The number of weeks the child has been in local authority care.

care_home_24h_per_week.description: Whether the child spends at least 24 hours per week at home while in local authority care.

hospital_weeks.description: The number of weeks the child has been in hospital or residential accommodation.

claimant_spends_on_child.description: Whether the claimant regularly spends money on the child (e.g. food, clothes, books) while the child is in hospital or residential accommodation.

is_fostered.description: Whether the child is fostered by the claimant.

council_pays_for_child.description: Whether the local council pays towards the child's accommodation or maintenance.

works_24_plus_hours.description: Whether the child works 24 or more hours per week in paid employment.

started_apprenticeship_in_england.description: Whether the child has started an apprenticeship in England.

receives_qualifying_benefits.description: Whether the child receives qualifying benefits in their own right, such as Universal Credit or Employment and Support Allowance.

test_case.agent_script:
=== YOUR SITUATION PROFILE ===
You live in the UK.
You are inquiring about your 3 children:
  - Alex is 4 years old and lives with you.
  - Blake is 16 years old and does not live with you.
  - Charlie is 15 years old and lives with you.

Here are the exact details of your circumstances to use when answering the agent's questions:

Regarding Alex:
  - Claimant lives in the UK
  - Child is 4 (under 16)
  - Child lives with claimant
  - Child is not in local authority care
  - Child in hospital for 12 weeks (within 12-week limit)
  - Child is not fostered
  - Child does not work 24+ hours/week
  - Child has not started an apprenticeship in England
  - Child does not receive qualifying benefits in their own right

Regarding Blake:
  - Claimant lives in the UK
  - Child is 16 and in the 20-week extension period
  - Claimant contributes £50.00/week towards child's upkeep (>= £26.05) and no other claimaint has priority
  - Child is not in local authority care
  - Child is not in hospital or residential accommodation
  - Child is not fostered
  - Child does not work 24+ hours/week
  - Child has not started an apprenticeship in England
  - Child does not receive qualifying benefits in their own right

Regarding Charlie:
  - Claimant lives in the UK
  - Child is 15 (under 16)
  - Child lives with claimant
  - Child is not in local authority care
  - Child is not in hospital or residential accommodation
  - Child is not fostered
  - Child does not work 24+ hours/week
  - Child has not started an apprenticeship in England
  - Child does not receive qualifying benefits in their own right
